name: Planar Inverted-F Antenna (microstrip)
description: A compact microstrip antenna consisting of a rectangular patch with a shorting pin and feed point, creating an inverted-F configuration. The shorting pin reduces the resonant length and provides impedance matching flexibility. Widely used in mobile communications due to its low profile and good impedance characteristics.

length.name: Patch Length
length.description: Length of the rectangular patch element

width.name: Patch Width
width.description: Width of the rectangular patch element

shortPosition.name: Short Pin Position
shortPosition.description: Distance from patch edge to shorting pin

feedPosition.name: Feed Position
feedPosition.description: Distance from shorting pin to feed point

dielectricConstant.name: Relative Permittivity
dielectricConstant.description: Relative dielectric constant of substrate material

designMethodology.overview: PIFA design involves determining patch dimensions for resonance, positioning the shorting pin and feed point for impedance matching, and optimizing for desired bandwidth and radiation characteristics.

steps.title: Calculate Effective Dielectric Constant
steps.description: Determine the effective permittivity accounting for fringing fields

steps.title: Estimate Initial Patch Length
steps.description: Calculate quarter-wavelength resonant length with end effect correction

steps.title: Determine Patch Width
steps.description: Set width for desired radiation resistance and bandwidth

steps.title: Position Shorting Pin
steps.description: Place shorting pin close to patch edge to minimize resonant length

steps.title: Calculate Feed Position
steps.description: Position feed point for 50-ohm impedance matching
steps.formula: Lf = L*sqrt(Rin/120), where Rin is desired input resistance

steps.title: Optimize Dimensions
steps.description: Fine-tune all parameters using electromagnetic simulation
steps.formula: Adjust L, W, Ls, Lf iteratively for S11 < -10 dB

references: Balanis, C.A., 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Wiley, 2016
Volakis, J.L., 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, McGraw-Hill, 2007
Stutzman, W.L. and Thiele, G.A., 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Wiley, 2012
